headline: Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model, dominates this cycle across the recommended educators.
body: The inner circle centers on Claude Fable 5, with Better Stack reporting it tops SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code while handling vision, long-context reasoning, and code generation across fullstack and CAD work [7]. Cyril Imhof frames the launch as a "gold rush," pairing benchmark wins on agentic coding and knowledge tasks against Opus 4.8, GPT 5.5, Codex, and Exploit Bench with the "Know Your Agent" (KYA) concept for deploying agents [4][5][6][8][9], and he extends the agent thread to long-horizon autonomy, delegation, agent governance, and enterprise digital identity [1][2]. AI Revolution notes Fable 5 spans software engineering, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity, with safety systems that restrict high-risk capability in cyber, biology, and chemistry [13]. Better Stack also covers operational items: a self-hosted NotebookLM alternative with podcast generation and grounded question-answering [10], a Chipotle-based workaround for free Claude coding [11], and an Instagram AI flaw granting account access through simple prompting [12].
